title: Getting Started
text: mod_tree() takes four arguments: path: directory containing R source files (defaults "R") app_fun: top-level launch function name (defaults "launch") ui_fun: UI function name (defaults "app_ui") server_fun: server function name (defaults "app_server") function parses every .R file path, identifies functions use NS() moduleServer() Shiny modules, builds call graph, performs depth-first traversal starting app_fun. result printed console. Use run_demo() launch bundled apps interactively can inspect source running mod_tree() .

title: Getting Started
text: simplest case: single module nested app_ui app_server, function names matching mod_tree()вЂ™s defaults. arguments beyond path needed. R/ directory app contains launch.R, app_ui.R, app_server.R, mod_greet.R. Point mod_tree() : root launch, two children app_ui app_server, carries single module leaf.
code: run_demo("01-default-names") mod_tree("inst/apps/01-default-names/R") в–€в”Ђlaunch в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_greet_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_greet_server

title: Getting Started
text: Real apps rarely use launch, app_ui, app_server verbatim. top-level functions different names, pass explicitly. app uses run_app() launcher, ui() interface, server() server logic. Calling mod_tree() without extra arguments start traversal function named "launch" doesnвЂ™t exist, producing empty tree. Supply correct names instead: tree structurally identical previous one; function names differ.
code: run_demo("02-custom-names") mod_tree(   "inst/apps/02-custom-names/R",   app_fun    = "run_app",   ui_fun     = "ui",   server_fun = "server" ) в–€в”Ђrun_app в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_counter_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђserver   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_counter_server

title: Modules
text: module calls child modules internally, tree gains depth. mod_tree() follows call graph recursively, nested relationships appear correct level. mod_dashboard parent module. UI server call mod_chart, making mod_chart grandchild app root: indentation reflects true call depth. mod_chart called modules, appear one level deeper.
code: run_demo("03-nested-modules") mod_tree("inst/apps/03-nested-modules/R") в–€в”Ђlaunch в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_dashboard_ui в”‚   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_chart_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_dashboard_server     в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_chart_server

title: Modules
text: every Shiny app uses modules. mod_tree() finds functions call NS() moduleServer(), tree collapses just three top-level functions. flat tree like useful signal: either app genuinely modules, module-detection pass missed something. latter case, check module functions actually contain NS() moduleServer() calls top level bodies.
code: run_demo("04-no-modules") mod_tree("inst/apps/04-no-modules/R") в–€в”Ђlaunch в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_server

title: Modules
text: number sibling modules grows, tree expands horizontally. app wires five modules directly app_ui app_server. order children branch follows order functions called source. mod_filter appears second second module called app_ui().
code: run_demo("05-many-modules") mod_tree("inst/apps/05-many-modules/R") в–€в”Ђlaunch в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_header_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_filter_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_table_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_chart_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_footer_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_header_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_filter_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_table_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_chart_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_footer_server

what: Using mod_tree_app() on a live object
title: Modules
text: mod_tree() reads files disk. mod_tree_app() inspects shiny.appobj memory instead; recovers server function directly app object identifies modules walking environment. result tree. Using five-module app example, build app object passing function references (evaluated calls): mod_tree_app() requires path argument. module structure comes serverвЂ™s enclosing environment, call shorter function names match defaults: root label defaults "app" shiny.appobj record name launch function. Pass app_fun override : tree structure unchanged; root label differs.
code: app <- shiny::shinyApp(ui = app_ui, server = app_server) mod_tree_app(app) в–€в”Ђapp в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_header_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_filter_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_table_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_chart_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_footer_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_header_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_filter_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_table_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_chart_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_footer_server mod_tree_app(app, app_fun = "launch")

title: Realistic App
text: previous examples deliberately minimal. Production apps include utilities alongside modules: logging helpers, data loaders, plot functions, non-module code. app . R/ directory contains 14 files, including logr_msg.R, scatter_plot.R, display_type.R, others define regular functions. called within modules modules . mod_tree() correctly excludes tree. Provide non-default function names app: Even though mod_scatter_display_server calls scatter_plot(), function appear tree. mod_tree() includes function output detected module one three named top-level functions.
code: run_demo("06-realistic-with-helpers") mod_tree(   "inst/apps/06-realistic-with-helpers/R",   app_fun    = "launch_app",   ui_fun     = "movies_ui",   server_fun = "movies_server" ) в–€в”Ђlaunch_app в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmovies_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_var_input_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_aes_input_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_scatter_display_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmovies_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_var_input_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_aes_input_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_scatter_display_server

what: The same tree from a live app object
title: Realistic App
text: mod_tree() works parsing files disk. mod_tree_app() takes different entry point: shiny.appobj created shiny::shinyApp(). Everything else вЂ” module detection, call-graph traversal, rendering вЂ” uses internals. use mod_tree_app() app, build app object first passing UI server functions unevaluated references (parentheses): call mod_tree_app() named arguments: output identical file-based call :
code: app <- shiny::shinyApp(ui = movies_ui, server = movies_server) mod_tree_app(   app,   app_fun    = "launch_app",   ui_fun     = "movies_ui",   server_fun = "movies_server" ) в–€в”Ђlaunch_app в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmovies_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_var_input_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_aes_input_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_scatter_display_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmovies_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_var_input_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_aes_input_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_scatter_display_server

previous_headings: The same tree from a live app object
title: Realistic App
text: ui passed shinyApp() parentheses вЂ” shinyApp(ui = movies_ui(), ...) вЂ” UI evaluated immediately HTML object function body longer recoverable app object. case, supply ui_fun character string. mod_tree_app() look function name serverвЂ™s enclosing environment use body UI branch: tree either way; difference whether mod_tree_app() can find UI function automatically.
code: app_pre <- shiny::shinyApp(ui = movies_ui(), server = movies_server)  mod_tree_app(   app_pre,   app_fun    = "launch_app",   ui_fun     = "movies_ui",   server_fun = "movies_server" )

title: Realistic App
text: mod_tree() statically parses R source files build print module call graph without running app. six scenarios covered represent range structures likely encounter: Default names вЂ” arguments beyond path needed app follows launch/app_ui/app_server conventions. Custom names вЂ” supply app_fun, ui_fun, server_fun top-level functions use different names. Nested modules вЂ” mod_tree() follows call graph recursively, child modules appear correct depth. modules вЂ” flat tree valid output; can also indicate detection miss worth investigating. Many modules вЂ” sibling modules fan horizontally, ordered call sequence source. Helper functions вЂ” regular functions called modules excluded tree; NS()- moduleServer()-bearing functions treated modules. Live app object вЂ” mod_tree_app() accepts shiny.appobj produces tree mod_tree() without touching file system; use app already memory path unavailable.

title: Test coverage and traceability
text: modtrees exposes two exported functions five internal helpers. run_demo() interactive launcher automated tests. correctness verified manually running bundled app.

title: Test coverage and traceability
text: Columns mark every function exercised test. Integration-level tests (test-mod_tree.R test-mod_tree_app.R) exercise multiple internal helpers single call; cells marked accordingly.

title: Test coverage and traceability
text: run_demo() automated tests. wraps shiny::shinyAppDir() path-resolution logic; correctness depends bundled apps inst/apps/ valid, implicitly verified whenever apps rendered manual inspection. mod_tree() integration tests test-mod_tree.R exercise five internal helpers combination. failure also fail corresponding unit test points interaction bug rather bug helper .

what: extract_func_def(): vector coercion with complex expressions
title: Test coverage and traceability
text: Found wild testing pffoundations package (July 2026, 35 R source files, ~20 modules). Symptom: mod_tree() crashes : Root cause. Line 17 R/extract_func_def.R: expr[[1]] complex call rather name, .character() returns character vector, scalar. %% check line 19 returns logical vector, && coerce scalar. Fix. Take first element comparison: Apply [1] guard line 28: Tests add. test covering complex left-hand-side expression currently causes crash: Status: Documented; fix regression test pending.
code: 'length = 3' in coercion to 'logical(1)' Calls: mod_tree -> extract_func_def op <- as.character(expr[[1]]) op <- as.character(expr[[1]])[1] if (op %in% c("<-", "=", "assign") && length(expr) == 3) { ... if (is.call(val) && as.character(val[[1]])[1] == "function") { test_that("returns NULL safely when expr[[1]] is a complex call", {   # e.g. a namespaced assignment: pkg::name <- function() {}   expr <- parse(text = "a::b <- function() {}")[[1]]   expect_null(extract_func_def(expr)) })

title: Test coverage and traceability
text: 38 tests span 7 functions across 6 test files. mod_tree() mod_tree_app() dedicated unit tests plus integration coverage internal helpers call. run_demo() untested automatically; manual verification inst/apps/ demos covers . One known bug extract_func_def() causes crash complex left-hand-side expressions; fix one-character change two lines.

title: Shiny Module Tree Utility
text: goal modtrees create plain-text tree views module namespace hierarchy statically parsing R source files.

title: Shiny Module Tree Utility
text: can install development version modtrees like :
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("mjfrigaard/modtrees")

title: Shiny Module Tree Utility
text: Example using ttdviewer source code files: Use mod_tree() function print module namespace tree:
code: library(modtrees) R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app_server.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app_ui.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ctr.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ inspect_plot.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ launch.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ load_tt_data.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ logr_msg.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_input.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_list.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_plot.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_report_desc.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_report_download.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_report_input.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod_table.R в”њв”Ђв”Ђ render_report.R в””в”Ђв”Ђ testthat.R  1 directory, 16 files mod_tree(   path = "R",    app_fun = "launch",    ui_fun = "app_ui",    server_fun = "app_server"   ) в–€в”Ђlaunch в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_input_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_report_input_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_report_desc_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_report_download_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_list_ui в”‚ в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_table_ui в”‚ в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_plot_ui в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђapp_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_input_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_report_input_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_report_desc_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_list_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_table_server   в”њв”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_plot_server   в””в”Ђв–€в”Ђmod_report_download_server

what: Build a nested tree from a call graph вЂ” build_tree
title: Build a nested tree from a call graph вЂ” build_tree
text: build_tree() performs depth-first traversal call graph starting given node, producing nested list structure suitable rendering.

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph вЂ” build_tree
code: build_tree(node, call_graph, visited = character(0))

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph вЂ” build_tree
text: node Character scalar; root function name. call_graph Named list element character vector function names called function. visited Character vector already-visited node names (used internally prevent cycles).

title: Build a nested tree from a call graph вЂ” build_tree
text: nested list elements name (character) children (list similar structures).

what: Extract function definitions from expression вЂ” extract_func_def
title: Extract function definitions from expression вЂ” extract_func_def
text: extract_func_def() hecks whether parsed expression top-level function assignment (e.g., name <- function(...) { ... }) returns function name body.

title: Extract function definitions from expression вЂ” extract_func_def
code: extract_func_def(expr)

title: Extract function definitions from expression вЂ” extract_func_def
text: expr parsed R expression.

title: Extract function definitions from expression вЂ” extract_func_def
text: list elements name (character) body (expression), NULL expression function definition.

what: Find all references to known functions within an expression вЂ” find_calls
title: Find all references to known functions within an expression вЂ” find_calls
text: find_calls() recursively walks parsed R expression collects names functions appear either direct calls (fun()) bare name references (fun).

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression вЂ” find_calls
code: find_calls(expr, known_names)

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression вЂ” find_calls
text: expr parsed R expression. known_names Character vector function names look .

title: Find all references to known functions within an expression вЂ” find_calls
text: Character vector unique matched function names.

code: hello()

what: Display a Shiny function module tree вЂ” mod_tree
title: Display a Shiny function module tree вЂ” mod_tree
text: mod_tree() statically parses R source files directory, detects Shiny modules (via calls shiny::NS() shiny::moduleServer()), builds call graph, prints plain-text tree showing module hierarchy.

title: Display a Shiny function module tree вЂ” mod_tree
code: mod_tree(   path = "R",   app_fun = "launch",   ui_fun = "app_ui",   server_fun = "app_server" )

title: Display a Shiny function module tree вЂ” mod_tree
text: path Character scalar; path directory containing R source files. Defaults "R". app_fun Character scalar; name top-level app launch function. Defaults "launch". ui_fun Character scalar; name UI function. Defaults "app_ui". server_fun Character scalar; name server function. Defaults "app_server".

title: Display a Shiny function module tree вЂ” mod_tree
text: Invisibly returns nested tree structure (list). tree printed console side effect.

title: Display a Shiny function module tree вЂ” mod_tree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ mod_tree("R") mod_tree("R", app_fun = "run_app", ui_fun = "ui", server_fun = "server") } # }

what: Display a module tree from a running Shiny app object вЂ” mod_tree_app
title: Display a module tree from a running Shiny app object вЂ” mod_tree_app
text: mod_tree_app() inspects shiny.appobj runtime rather parsing source files disk. extracts server function via shiny::shinyApp()'s internal serverFuncSource slot, collects functions visible server's enclosing environment, identifies Shiny modules uses_ns(), renders plain-text tree mod_tree().

title: Display a module tree from a running Shiny app object вЂ” mod_tree_app
code: mod_tree_app(app, app_fun = "app", ui_fun = NULL, server_fun = NULL)

title: Display a module tree from a running Shiny app object вЂ” mod_tree_app
text: app shiny.appobj, returned shiny::shinyApp(). app_fun Character scalar; label used root node tree. Defaults "app". app object store name launch function, display label . ui_fun Character scalar NULL; name UI function. NULL (default), mod_tree_app() attempts recover UI function automatically. Auto-detection succeeds ui passed shiny::shinyApp() unevaluated function reference (e.g. shinyApp(ui = app_ui, ...)). ui pre-evaluated (e.g. shinyApp(ui = app_ui(), ...)), supply function name explicitly UI branch can included tree. server_fun Character scalar NULL; name server function. NULL (default), name inferred comparing function bodies visible server's environment.

title: Display a module tree from a running Shiny app object вЂ” mod_tree_app
text: Invisibly returns nested tree structure (list). tree printed console side effect.

title: Display a module tree from a running Shiny app object вЂ” mod_tree_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ app <- shiny::shinyApp(ui = app_ui, server = app_server) mod_tree_app(app)  # pre-evaluated UI requires explicit ui_fun app2 <- shiny::shinyApp(ui = app_ui(), server = app_server) mod_tree_app(app2, ui_fun = "app_ui")  # non-default names mod_tree_app(app, app_fun = "launch", ui_fun = "movies_ui", server_fun = "movies_server") } # }

what: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art вЂ” render_tree
title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art вЂ” render_tree
text: render_tree() takes nested tree structure (produced build_tree()) returns character vector lines using box-drawing characters style lobstr::ast().

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art вЂ” render_tree
code: render_tree(tree, prefix = "", is_last = TRUE, is_root = TRUE)

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art вЂ” render_tree
text: tree nested list name children elements. prefix Character scalar; current indentation prefix (used internally recursion). is_last Logical; whether node last sibling. is_root Logical; whether node root.

title: Render Shiny module tree as plain-text ASCII art вЂ” render_tree
text: Character vector formatted lines.

what: Launch a demo Shiny app вЂ” run_demo
title: Launch a demo Shiny app вЂ” run_demo
text: Launches one demo Shiny applications bundled modtrees. called without arguments runs original Movies Review app (inst/app/). Pass folder name inst/apps/ launch one targeted mod_tree() test apps.

title: Launch a demo Shiny app вЂ” run_demo
code: run_demo(app = NULL, options = list())

title: Launch a demo Shiny app вЂ” run_demo
text: app Character scalar; name app folder inside inst/apps/, e.g. "01-default-names". Pass NULL (default) launch original Movies Review demo inst/app/. options named list options passed shiny::shinyAppDir(), e.g. list(port = 8080).

title: Launch a demo Shiny app вЂ” run_demo
text: Shiny application object (invisibly).

title: Launch a demo Shiny app вЂ” run_demo
code: if (interactive()) {   # original demo   run_demo()    # targeted test apps   run_demo("01-default-names")   run_demo("02-custom-names")   run_demo("03-nested-modules")   run_demo("04-no-modules")   run_demo("05-many-modules")   run_demo("06-realistic-with-helpers") }
